Title: A microbial supply chain for production of the anti-cancer drug vinblastine

Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ERG20 downregulation was used to improve the GPP:FPP ratio and increase monoterpene precursor supply. 2. Dynamic knockdown of ERG20 improved de novo strictosidine production. 3. Low-FPP-activity ERG20(F96W,N127W) fused to tCroGES helped redirect flux to geraniol.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Reducing native FPP synthesis increases precursor availability for geraniol and downstream MIA production.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: dynamic promoter replacement; variant overexpression; fusion design; metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: glucose-repressible dynamic control in SC/YPD media
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: PHXT3 for knockdown; PICI1 and other engineered promoters for expression variants
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Excess reduction of sterol-pathway flux may impair growth balance.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Central flux-control lever for microbial monoterpene alkaloid engineering.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Native yeast precursor-supply engineering rather than plant pathway catalysis.

Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Dynamic downregulation of ERG9 was used to reduce GPP consumption and improve geraniol supply. 2. This engineering contributed to de novo strictosidine production from glucose.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Redirects isoprenoid flux away from sterol synthesis toward MIA precursor formation.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: dynamic promoter replacement; pathway engineering; metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential growth/sterol defects if too strongly repressed.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful chassis-engineering target for terpene-derived alkaloid production.

Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Overexpression of AgrGPPS2 increased GPP supply for geraniol synthesis. 2. Additional copies were later integrated to boost one-pot vindoline and catharanthine production.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances precursor supply for monoterpene branch and downstream MIA production.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: overexpression; promoter swapping; extra-copy integration; metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Applied_Promoters: PMLS1 or constitutive promoters depending strain design
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A strong heterologous precursor-supply enzyme for terpene alkaloid chassis optimization.

Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Geraniol synthase expression was initially poor by targeted proteomics. 2. Fusion to ERG20(F96W,N127W) was used to redirect GPP toward geraniol. 3. Additional copies supported improved de novo vindoline/catharanthine production.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Improves geraniol supply feeding the strictosidine pathway.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: targeted proteomics; fusion engineering; extra-copy integration; metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: plastidial in plant, engineered in yeast
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Truncated form used for yeast engineering.

Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Overexpression of CroTDC converted tryptophan to tryptamine for de novo strictosidine synthesis. 2. This was part of the final precursor-supply optimization leading to glucose-based strictosidine production.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increases tryptamine supply for strictosidine formation.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: overexpression in production strains; metabolite profiling
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for establishing de novo indole entry into MIA pathways.

Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CroCPR was co-expressed in each module to support multiple plant P450 enzymes. 2. Extra copy integration of CroCPR with GO and GS was used to improve post-strictosidine conversion.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Supports activity of heterologous P450 enzymes required for MIA biosynthesis.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Essential support component for plant P450-heavy pathways in yeast.

Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CroCYB5 was co-expressed with CroCPR to support cytochrome P450 enzymes across pathway modules.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Facilitates efficient P450 function in engineered yeast MIA pathways.

Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Domain-shuffled SGD hybrids showed RseSGD domain 3 was required for activity in yeast. 2. Maintaining R3+R4 from RseSGD gave highest THA production.
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Defines structural features needed for functional SGD activity in yeast.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: domain swapping; THA production assay; localization microscopy
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because the hybrid engineering itself was a core experimental focus supporting downstream pathway enablement.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Condenses secologanin and tryptamine in a Pictet–Spengler-type reaction to form strictosidine.
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: terminal step of the strictosidine module / gateway to downstream MIAs
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Vacuole or endoplasmic reticulum membrane in yeast; vacuolar targeting relevant
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a rate-limiting step for strictosidine production
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. STR was identified as a major bottleneck because secologanin remained unused in the medium. 2. Overexpression of high-copy tCroSTR substantially improved strictosidine titre. 3. Full-length CroSTR outperformed truncated STR variants and enabled ~80% conversion of fed secologanin, showing localization-dependent activity.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes strictosidine accumulation; full-length CroSTR strongly increases strictosidine production in yeast.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: precursor feeding; homologue screening; truncation comparison; overexpression; metabolite profiling; fluorescence localization
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: engineered expression in yeast strictosidine platform strains
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: mixed engineered constitutive promoters; high-copy plasmid expression for tCroSTR
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: feeding with secologanin and tryptamine; SC and YPD media
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Common precursor for more than 3,000 MIAs including anticancer alkaloid precursors
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Key engineering target for improving MIA gateway flux; full-length enzyme and proper compartmentalization should be prioritized.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Truncated versions showed poor activity likely due to mislocalization.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CroSTR was superior among screened STR homologues; acidic vacuolar environment or trafficking may support activity.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: targeted proteomics PXD024976 (strain-level pathway optimization context)

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes 8-hydroxygeraniol toward 8-oxogeranial in the iridoid precursor pathway.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: forms immediate precursor / supplies precursor pool
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as one of the main bottlenecks upstream of strictosidine
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Feeding analysis indicated 8HGO and ISY were main bottlenecks. 2. Screening of 8HGO variants identified Vmi8HGO-A as the most efficient enzyme. 3. Some 8HGO-B homologues negatively affected production, likely by catalysing the reverse reaction.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Vmi8HGO-A improves conversion from 8-hydroxygeraniol to downstream iridoids and increases strictosidine production.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: precursor feeding; combinatorial enzyme screening; integration; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Cro8HGO-B and Ptr8HGO-B likely catalysed reverse reaction; native yeast oxidoreductases also showed background activity.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Use Vmi8HGO-A rather than less favorable homologues to improve iridoid flux in microbial MIA production.
Pathway_Genes.Gene_Accession_Number: Vmi8HGO-A sequence identified from transcriptome; accession NA

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Reductively cyclizes 8-oxogeranial toward nepetalactol-type iridoid intermediates.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a main bottleneck in the strictosidine module
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Feeding analysis indicated ISY was a major bottleneck. 2. Combinatorial screening identified NcaISY combined with NcaML-PLA as the best-performing cyclization system. 3. Integration of the optimal pair improved downstream secologanin/strictosidine pathway performance.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: NcaISY improves iridoid precursor formation and boosts downstream strictosidine flux.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: precursor feeding; combinatorial screening; pathway integration; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Cyclase partner choice strongly affected output.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Optimize ISY together with partner cyclase for microbial iridoid engineering.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Assists/catalyses cyclization of activated iridoid intermediate generated from 8-oxogeranial toward nepetalactol-related products.
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: reactive iridoid intermediate from ISY reaction
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: cyclized iridoid intermediate leading to loganic acid/loganin/secologanin
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Screening of cyclase partners showed NcaML-PLA with NcaISY gave the highest titres of downstream intermediates and strictosidine. 2. This established cyclase choice as important for flux through the iridoid branch.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances cyclization efficiency and increases downstream strictosidine-pathway intermediates.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not a classic vinblastine pathway enzyme from C. roseus but a supporting heterologous enzyme used to optimize pathway refactoring.

Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: gateway from strictosidine to downstream alkaloid branches
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydrolyses strictosidine to strictosidine aglycone, enabling entry into downstream MIA scaffolds.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies committed intermediate for downstream target products
Pathway_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Nucleus in yeast for active RseSGD
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key gateway step; functional enzyme choice decisive in yeast
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CroSGD was non-functional in yeast for detectable downstream THA formation, whereas RseSGD enabled THA production. 2. RseSGD localized to the nucleus, consistent with active complex/channeling. 3. Domain 3 of RseSGD was required for activity, and R3+R4 integrity maximized THA production. 4. RseSGD was incorporated into de novo tabersonine pathway construction.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enables functional conversion of strictosidine into downstream MIA branch products in yeast.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: homologue screening; side-product assay with THAS; domain swapping; fluorescence localization; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: feeding secologanin and tryptamine for THA assay
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: CroSGD showed diffuse cytoplasmic localization and poor function in yeast; RseSGD solved the gateway bottleneck.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Choose RseSGD-like variants or engineer CroSGD domains/localization for robust downstream MIA biosynthesis in microbes.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates tabersonine at C16 to form 16-hydroxytabersonine.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Extra copies improved vindoline, indicating important control point
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. The vinblastine module produced vindoline from tabersonine and catharanthine feeding. 2. Overexpression of T16H2 together with 16OMT gave the highest (>2-fold) increase in vindoline titre among tested additions. 3. T16H2 was selected for de novo vindoline module construction.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes vindoline biosynthesis from tabersonine in yeast.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: module reconstruction; precursor feeding; extra-copy overexpression; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: T16H2 outperformed T16H1 in the engineering workflow.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Prioritize T16H2 and co-balance downstream vindoline enzymes for microbial vindoline production.

Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Extra copies with T16H2 strongly improved vindoline titre
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Extra copies of 16OMT together with T16H2 yielded the strongest vindoline improvement. 2. The accumulation of 16-hydroxytabersonine/16-methoxytabersonine confirmed flux through this portion of the pathway.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enhances vindoline biosynthesis when co-optimized with T16H2.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: module reconstruction; precursor feeding; extra-copy overexpression; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Used in de novo vindoline strain MIA-CW-1.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes 16-methoxytabersonine in the pathway toward hydroxylated reduced vindoline intermediates.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Potential bottleneck supported by intermediate accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Metabolite analysis detected strong accumulation of 16-hydroxytabersonine and 16-methoxytabersonine. 2. These data indicated T3O as a potential bottleneck in the vindoline module.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Insufficient T3O activity constrains vindoline accumulation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: module reconstruction; metabolite profiling of accumulated intermediates
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Potentially one of the weakest P450 steps in the vindoline branch.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts dihydroprecondylocarpine acetate-derived intermediate to tabersonine.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Low expression indicated likely bottleneck in tabersonine branch
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. De novo integration of the tabersonine module enabled tabersonine production from glucose and tryptophan. 2. Proteomics indicated relatively low expression of tabersonine synthase in strain MIA-DJ. 3. An extra copy of CroTS was included in later improved strains, supporting TS as a limiting step.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes tabersonine formation; insufficient expression restricts flux to vindoline branch.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: module integration; metabolite profiling; untargeted proteomics; extra-copy engineering
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Catharanthine synthase outcompeted or outperformed TS in shared-precursor branch context.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts dihydroprecondylocarpine acetate-derived intermediate to catharanthine.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. In dual-module yeast, catharanthine accumulated more strongly than tabersonine, indicating stronger expression or catalytic efficiency of CS. 2. Galactose-inducible expression of CS enabled one-pot co-production of catharanthine with vindoline. 3. CS competes with TS for a shared precursor, affecting branch allocation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes catharanthine accumulation and diverts shared precursor away from tabersonine/vindoline branch.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: module integration; inducible expression; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Competition with tabersonine/vindoline branch reduces availability of shared precursor for vindoline production.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Inducible or stage-specific CS expression is useful to balance co-production of catharanthine and vindoline.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts strictosidine aglycone-derived intermediate toward geissoschizine.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Extra copies of CroGS were introduced to enhance conversion of unstable strictosidine aglycone. 2. GS was part of the limiting tabersonine/catharanthine module improved in later strains.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Improves flux from strictosidine aglycone into downstream tabersonine/catharanthine products.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: module engineering; extra-copy integration; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Introduced specifically to improve handling of unstable strictosidine aglycone.

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Oxidizes geissoschizine in the pathway toward stemmadenine derivatives.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GO was included in the functional tabersonine/catharanthine module. 2. Extra copy integration of GO together with GS and CPR was used to improve conversion of strictosidine aglycone toward downstream products.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes downstream branch flux to tabersonine/catharanthine.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: module engineering; extra-copy integration; metabolite profiling

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Suggested to catalyse oxidative coupling of vindoline and catharanthine toward vinblastine/anhydrovinblastine-related product formation.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PRX1-containing yeast strains did not produce detectable vinblastine. 2. Confocal microscopy indicated CroPRX1 was not properly expressed/folded. 3. Failure of PRX1 was a major reason vinblastine required ex vivo chemical coupling.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: No functional vinblastine biosynthesis achieved in yeast due to PRX1 expression/folding limitation.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: module integration; precursor feeding; confocal microscopy; metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors suggest a suitable homologue or peroxisomal compartmentalization may eventually solve this step.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Major future engineering target for complete in vivo vinblastine biosynthesis.
